sessionName: Let's Roll This Thing Out
sessionDescription: Learn a new roll and a twist to get you started with this program.

exercises.name: A-Frame
exercises.description: This exercise improves your ability to reach overhead as well as lengthening the back of your legs from your hips to your heels.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), press hips up as high as possible.
Allow a slight bend in the knees.
Body forms an upside-down V (A-frame/downward dog shape).
key_cues: Push hips UP and BACK as far as possible.
Allow slight knee bend — don't force straight legs if hamstrings are tight.
Press the floor away with the hands — engage the scapulae.
This is the entry position before Bear and Frogger work.

exercises.name: Prone Bent Arm Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Stretch out your chest and get it ready to move.

how_to: Lie face down. Extend one arm out to the side at shoulder height.
Use the other hand to gently push yourself up, rotating the chest open.
key_cues: The stretch is felt in the chest and front of the shoulder.
Keep the hip of the rotating side pressed down.
Breathe into the chest — don't hold your breath.

exercises.name: Quadruped Twist
exercises.description: This exercise not only works on spinal rotation but also on the coordination between your shoulder and spine to make this motion strong and stable.

how_to: In table top, place one hand behind your head.
Rotate that elbow down toward the opposite hand, then open up toward the ceiling.
key_cues: The rotation comes from the thoracic spine — try not to rotate from the lower back.
Keep the planted hand firmly on the floor.
Breathe in as you open up, breathe out as you close.

exercises.name: Basic Spiderman
exercises.description: This variation of the Bear works on your chest, shoulder, and hip mobility as well as cross body coordination between your upper and lower body

how_to: From Bear or plank position, step one foot up outside the same-side hand.
Lower hip toward the floor to open the groin.
Alternate sides as you travel forward.
key_cues: Foot lands outside the hand — don't let it fall inside.
Drive the hip toward the floor on each step.
Keep the trailing leg extended and active.

exercises.name: Side Roll
exercises.description: This movement will have you take your body to the side as you roll your legs up over your body.

how_to: From standing or kneeling, tuck and roll over one shoulder to the opposite hip.
Use the arms to guide the roll — don't land on the shoulder point.
key_cues: Roll on the BACK of the shoulder, not the point — protect the joint.
Tuck chin to chest before initiating.
Stay relaxed — tension makes rolls harder and more jarring.
The roll should feel like a smooth diagonal across the back.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Combo A
exercises.description: This combo will have you practicing different twisting patterns.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

sessionName: Underswitch to Squat and Leg Sweep
sessionDescription: Practice the Underswitch to Squat along with a leg sweep then play with a combination.

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat
exercises.description: This movement starts with your hands behind and legs in front. This allows you to keep your feet flat as you bring your hips forward to meet them.

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Quadruped Wag Tail
exercises.description: This sidebending motion activates your core in a unique way, the muscles alongside your spine and obliques are used and prepare them well

how_to: In table top, shift the hips side to side — like a dog wagging its tail.
The spine side-bends as the hips move left and right.
key_cues: Keep the movement rhythmic and relaxed.
Try to feel the side-bend through the whole spine, not just the lower back.
Great for activating the lateral core (obliques).

exercises.name: Table Top Twist
exercises.description: This motion takes you through spinal sidebending and rotation, along with a lateral stretch at the hips.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), thread one arm under the body.
Rotate the thoracic spine to lower that shoulder toward the floor.
Return and repeat on the other side.
key_cues: The rotation is thoracic — keep the lower back still.
Let the shoulder lower as far as comfortable.
Breathe out as you thread under.

exercises.name: Seated Underswitch to Squat
exercises.description: This combo will have you pull your leg underneath you from a sitting position into a squat.

how_to: From a seated position, perform an underswitch (rotate hips to bring legs through under the body).
Land in a squat as the legs come through.
key_cues: The underswitch is a hip rotation — let the hips lead.
Keep one hand planted through the switch for support.
Aim to land softly in squat with feet flat.

exercises.name: Squat Leg Sweep
exercises.description: Squat Leg Sweep

how_to: From a deep squat, sweep one leg out to the side along the floor.
The leg extends and sweeps in an arc as you shift weight to the other side.
key_cues: Keep the planted foot flat and the planted knee tracking over toes.
The sweep is fluid — let momentum carry the leg.
Engage the inner thigh of the sweeping leg as it extends.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Combo B
exercises.description: This combo focuses on twisting the body while only using one hand and one leg.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

sessionName: Froggers and Spins
sessionDescription: Practice two versions of the Frogger and play with a Cross Hand Spin to A-Frame.

exercises.name: Frog Stretch
exercises.description: Open up your hips for better mobility.

how_to: Start on hands and knees and spread knees wide apart.
Lower hips back and down between the knees.
Feet point out in line with the knees.
key_cues: Keep feet and ankles in line with knees — don't let them roll in.
Rock gently forward and back to explore the range.
Breathe and relax — let gravity increase the stretch over time.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Fold
exercises.description: This exercise keeps your back in a good strong position and teaches you how to stretch your legs without bending and straining your back

how_to: Sit with legs extended straight in front.
Fold forward from the hips — reach toward the feet.
key_cues: Fold from the HIPS, not the lower back — maintain a long spine as long as possible.
Let gravity increase the stretch — don't force the fold.
Flex the feet (toes toward you) to increase the hamstring stretch.

exercises.name: Wrists - Palms Down, Fingers Facing Knees
exercises.description: Get your wrists ready to safely bear some weight.

how_to: Kneel or stand and place hands on the floor with palms down, fingers pointing back toward your knees.
Gently shift weight forward and backward over the hands.
key_cues: Go slowly — this targets the commonly tight wrist extensors.
Breathe into any tightness — don't force.
Work incrementally closer to the knees as flexibility improves.

exercises.name: Frogger Alternative Leg Hop
exercises.description: Rather than walking one leg at a time, this variation of the Frogger will find you alternating your feet as you hop forward.

how_to: Instead of hopping both feet together, alternate legs with each rep.
One foot hops forward at a time.
key_cues: Challenges unilateral leg control and balance.
Keep the upper body stable as only one leg moves.

exercises.name: Straight Leg Frogger Open and Closed
exercises.description: In this variation of the Frogger you will extend your legs and keep them straight throughout the movement.

how_to: Perform the Frogger with legs kept straight throughout.
Requires flexible hamstrings to pike forward.
key_cues: Hamstring flexibility is the primary limiter — don't force it.
Keep the pull mechanic: arms and torso initiate, legs follow.
Engage the quads to keep the knees from bending.
benefits: Rotator cuff and knee strength.
Hip mobility increase.
Spinal strength and controlled mobility.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Combo A
exercises.description: This combo will challenge your twisting control while working with familiar movements.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

sessionName: Transformer and Bent Arm Floating Table Top
sessionDescription: Practice the Transformer and Bent Arm Floating Table Top then work on putting them together.

exercises.name: Wrist Circles
exercises.description: Our wrists can take on a lot of force in bodyweight exercise, these circles help them to recover.

how_to: Extend arms forward or overhead.
Make slow circles with the wrists — clockwise then counterclockwise.
key_cues: Make the circles as large as possible to access full wrist range.
Do both directions — wrists often have asymmetric mobility.
Can be done in weight-bearing position (on hands) for greater effect.

exercises.name: Standing Twist
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation that helps release tension throughout the spine, shoulders, and arms.

how_to: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Rotate the torso side to side, arms swinging freely.
key_cues: Let the arms be completely relaxed — they swing from the torso rotation.
Keep feet flat and knees soft.
Gradually increase the rotation range as the spine warms up.

exercises.name: Extended Side Angle Pose
exercises.description: This classic yoga pose is great for lengthening the entire body as you sidebend and rotate through your back and into your hips and legs

how_to: Stand in a wide lunge, front knee at 90°.
Lower the front forearm to the front thigh (or hand to floor).
Reach the top arm overhead, creating a long diagonal line.
key_cues: Stack the hips — don't let the top hip collapse forward.
Reach actively through the top arm to lengthen the side body.
Keep the back leg strong and active.

exercises.name: Transformer
exercises.description: Transformer

how_to: A multi-position transition movement that flows between ground positions.
Typically: prone → side-lying → seated → supine and back.
key_cues: Move smoothly and continuously — no pausing between positions.
Use the hands for support only when necessary.
Think of it as a flowing shape-change, not a series of stops.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Floating Table Top
exercises.description: A bent arm version of a very familiar movement.

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Combo B
exercises.description: This challenging combo will test your ability to go from a straight arm position to bent arm hold.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

sessionName: A-Frame Variations
sessionDescription: Practice some A-Frame variations and finish by stringing them together into a combination.

Body forms an upside-down V (A-frame/downward dog shape).

key_cues: Keep the movement rhythmic and relaxed.
Try to feel the side-bend through the whole spine, not just the lower back.

Rotate that elbow down toward the opposite hand, then open up toward the ceiling.
key_cues: The rotation comes from the thoracic spine — try not to rotate from the lower back.
Keep the planted hand firmly on the floor.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Floating Table Top

exercises.name: Forearm Floating Table Top
exercises.description: This movement teaches you a stable back position with bracing while on your elbows. This is very important and becomes automatic as you practice and move on to locomotions

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Combo A
exercises.description: Work your way to the floor from the A-Frame position.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.
Focus on controlled lowering and rising through various ground shapes.
key_cues: Slow and deliberate — resist gravity on the way down.
Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

sessionName: Leg Thread and Wipers
sessionDescription: Today you will learn two new movements and then play with putting them together.

exercises.name: Standing Twist
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation that helps release tension throughout the spine, shoulders, and arms.

how_to: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Rotate the torso side to side, arms swinging freely.
key_cues: Let the arms be completely relaxed — they swing from the torso rotation.
Keep feet flat and knees soft.
Gradually increase the rotation range as the spine warms up.

exercises.name: General Kneeling Lunge
exercises.description: A cornerstone exercise for improving your hip flexor mobility as well as learning how to differentiate your spine, pelvis, and hip motions

how_to: Kneel with one knee on the floor, the other foot planted forward.
Shift hips forward gently to stretch the hip flexor of the back leg.
Keep torso upright.
key_cues: Posteriorly tilt the pelvis (tuck the tailbone) to increase the hip flexor stretch.
Don't lean forward — stay tall to maximize the stretch.
Breathe into the front of the hip and inner thigh.

exercises.name: Quadruped Wag Tail
exercises.description: This sidebending motion activates your core in a unique way, the muscles alongside your spine and obliques are used and prepare them well

how_to: In table top, shift the hips side to side — like a dog wagging its tail.
The spine side-bends as the hips move left and right.
key_cues: Keep the movement rhythmic and relaxed.
Try to feel the side-bend through the whole spine, not just the lower back.
Great for activating the lateral core (obliques).

exercises.name: Forearm Leg Thread
exercises.description: Practice pulling one leg under you while bracing yourself on your forearm.

how_to: From a ground position, thread one leg under the body supported on forearms.
The leg slides through the space between the arms and the floor.
key_cues: Keep forearms flat and elbows under shoulders.
The threading leg stays close to the floor.
Control the movement — no dropping or flopping.

exercises.name: Knee Wiper to Kneeling Lunge
exercises.description: Practice a twisting movement on your knees.

how_to: From seated, let knees fall side to side (wipers) to loosen hips.
Transition into a kneeling lunge from the last wiper position.
key_cues: The wipers should be relaxed — gravity drops the knees.
Transition smoothly from the last wiper into the lunge.
In the lunge: posteriorly tilt pelvis to deepen the hip flexor stretch.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Combo B
exercises.description: Combine a leg thread to end up in a kneeling lunge position.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.
Focus on controlled lowering and rising through various ground shapes.
key_cues: Slow and deliberate — resist gravity on the way down.
Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

sessionName: 3 Point Bridge and 9090
sessionDescription: In today's session you'll practice the 3-Point Bridge and 9090 Supine Rotation to 9090 moves.

exercises.name: Kneeling Back Flexion to Prone Lying Back Extension
exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).
Then lie prone (face down) and press up into a cobra/back extension.
Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Modified Pigeon Stretch
exercises.description: Moving your hips freely comes primarily from the flexibility of your hip flexors and rotators. This exercise works those deep rotators well

how_to: From a lunge, bring the front shin across the body (shin parallel to the front of the mat).
Lower the hips toward the floor.
Can be done lying on back (figure-four / supine pigeon) if floor pigeon is too intense.
key_cues: The deeper the shin angle (more parallel), the more intense the stretch.
Keep hips square — don't let one side roll up.
Breathe into the outer hip and glute.

exercises.name: Table Top Twist
exercises.description: This motion takes you through spinal sidebending and rotation, along with a lateral stretch at the hips.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), thread one arm under the body.
Rotate the thoracic spine to lower that shoulder toward the floor.
Return and repeat on the other side.
key_cues: The rotation is thoracic — keep the lower back still.
Let the shoulder lower as far as comfortable.
Breathe out as you thread under.

exercises.name: 3-Point Bridge
exercises.description: Build stability and flexibility for bending and twisting.

how_to: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat.
Press hips up into a bridge.
Extend one leg straight out, hold for a moment, lower and switch.
key_cues: Drive through the heel of the planted foot.
Keep hips level when one leg is extended — don't tilt.
Squeeze the glutes at the top of the bridge.

exercises.name: 9090 Supine Rotation
exercises.description: This movement pattern teaches you how to do a controlled "fall" backward using your trunk and arms.

how_to: Lie on your side with hips stacked at 90° and knees bent at 90°.
Open the top arm across the body, rotating the thoracic spine.
Let the arm reach toward the floor on the opposite side.
key_cues: Keep the hips stacked — don't let the bottom hip rotate.
The rotation is purely thoracic (upper back).
Breathe out as you open — let the chest follow the arm.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo A
exercises.description: Practice combining familiar movements while in a supine position.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

sessionName: Merry Go Round and Superhero
sessionDescription: Practice the Merry Go Round and the Superhero hold.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Fold
exercises.description: This exercise keeps your back in a good strong position and teaches you how to stretch your legs without bending and straining your back

how_to: Sit with legs extended straight in front.
Fold forward from the hips — reach toward the feet.
key_cues: Fold from the HIPS, not the lower back — maintain a long spine as long as possible.
Let gravity increase the stretch — don't force the fold.
Flex the feet (toes toward you) to increase the hamstring stretch.

exercises.name: Seated Twist
exercises.description: Rotation is another key to moving more comfortably. Activities that have you twist and turn feel so much better when your back can rotate well.

how_to: Sit tall, cross one leg over, place the foot flat on the floor.
Rotate the torso toward the bent knee, using the elbow for leverage.
key_cues: Lengthen the spine first — then rotate.
Breathe out to deepen the twist.
Keep both sitting bones on the floor.

exercises.name: Kneeling Back Flexion to Prone Lying Back Extension
exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).
Then lie prone (face down) and press up into a cobra/back extension.
Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Merry Go Round Basic
exercises.description: Merry Go Round Basic

how_to: From a low position (seated or Crab), rotate the body around a fixed point (usually one hand).
The body sweeps around in a circle.
key_cues: The planted hand is the pivot — keep it grounded.
Sweep with the hips — let the legs follow.
Keep hips off the ground throughout the rotation.

exercises.name: Superhero
exercises.description: Practice a hold in order to strengthen your lower back

how_to: A dynamic movement flowing between prone (chest down) positions.
Often involves pressing up from prone, transitioning over hands.
key_cues: Stay fluid — connect each position without pausing.
Engage the core to support the lower back in prone positions.
Move with intention — each shape should be clear before moving to the next.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo B
exercises.description: Practice controlled spins and rotations going from a supine to prone position.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

sessionName: Sumo Frogger and Single Leg Sumo Squat
sessionDescription: Practice the Sumo Frogger and learn a new variation of the Sumo Squat.

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat
exercises.description: This movement starts with your hands behind and legs in front. This allows you to keep your feet flat as you bring your hips forward to meet them.

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Half Cossack Reach
exercises.description: This upper and lower body coordination movement combines a lateral trunk stretch with the Cossack Squat.

how_to: Stand in a wide stance.
Shift weight to one side into a lateral lunge, extending the other leg straight.
Reach the arm of the extended-leg side across the body or overhead.
key_cues: Keep the heel of the bent-knee leg flat on the floor.
The extended leg stays long — flex that foot.
The reach amplifies the side-body and inner-thigh stretch.

exercises.name: Frog Stretch
exercises.description: Open up your hips for better mobility.

how_to: Start on hands and knees and spread knees wide apart.
Lower hips back and down between the knees.
Feet point out in line with the knees.
key_cues: Keep feet and ankles in line with knees — don't let them roll in.
Rock gently forward and back to explore the range.
Breathe and relax — let gravity increase the stretch over time.

exercises.name: Sumo Frogger
exercises.description: This Frogger locomotion variant challenges your hip mobility in a different way as you work to keep your feet and knees as wide apart as you can.

how_to: Start in a wide-stance squat with feet turned out.
Hands reach forward between the wide legs.
Perform the Frogger pulling motion with a wide hip stance throughout.
key_cues: Wide stance demands more groin and hip abductor mobility.
Keep knees tracking over toes even in the wide stance.
Don't collapse the knees inward as you land.
benefits: Elbow stability.
Knee stability.
Increased hip mobility and strength through abduction and flexion.

exercises.name: Single Leg Sumo Squat
exercises.description: Time to pretend you're a sumo wrestler with this single leg squat variation.

how_to: Stand in a wide sumo stance.
Shift weight to one side, bending that knee deeply while the other leg stays extended.
Alternate sides.
key_cues: Keep the bent knee tracking over the toes — don't let it cave in.
The extended leg stays long and straight.
Maintain an upright torso throughout.

exercises.name: Big In Japan (Sumo) Combo A
exercises.description: This sumo-related combination will improve your hip strength and leg balance.

how_to: A wide-stance, sumo-focused combination incorporating deep hip mobility movements.
Typically includes Sumo Frogger, Single Leg Sumo Squat, and related wide-stance flows.
key_cues: Wide stance throughout — maintain groin engagement.
Keep knees tracking over toes even in the wide positions.
Think of the sumo stance as a stable, powerful base.

sessionName: Long Leg Monkey and Deep Lunge
sessionDescription: Practice the Long Leg Monkey, a twist, and a deep lunge.

Keep torso upright.

Breathe into the front of the hip and inner thigh.

how_to: Stand in a wide stance.
Shift weight to one side into a lateral lunge, extending the other leg straight.
Reach the arm of the extended-leg side across the body or overhead.
key_cues: Keep the heel of the bent-knee leg flat on the floor.
The extended leg stays long — flex that foot.

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.

exercises.name: Basic Long Leg Monkey
exercises.description: In this variation of the Monkey you will extend your leg and while staying low and transition laterally into a squat via the Monkey.

how_to: Perform the Monkey with straight legs throughout.
Legs stay extended as you hop sideways.
key_cues: Requires hamstring flexibility to keep legs straight in lateral movement.
Drive the hip crease forward as you shift weight onto hands.
Don't compensate by bending the knees — work the range gradually.
benefits: All Basic Monkey benefits plus increased hip strength and mobility.

exercises.name: Deep Lunge
exercises.description: Variation of a general lunge that will have your leg extended in back.

how_to: Step into a long lunge with the back knee on the floor.
Sink the hips as low as possible toward the floor.
key_cues: Front shin should be vertical — knee over ankle.
Drive the back hip toward the floor.
Arms can reach overhead to increase the stretch through the hip flexor and spine.

exercises.name: Big In Japan (Sumo) Combo B
exercises.description: Combine familiar leg movements while staying close to the floor

how_to: A wide-stance, sumo-focused combination incorporating deep hip mobility movements.
Typically includes Sumo Frogger, Single Leg Sumo Squat, and related wide-stance flows.
key_cues: Wide stance throughout — maintain groin engagement.
Keep knees tracking over toes even in the wide positions.
Think of the sumo stance as a stable, powerful base.

sessionName: Underswitch to Squat and Leg Sweeps
sessionDescription: Practice the Underswitch to Squat and Leg Sweep then play with the Twisted Sister (Rotation) flow, Combo B.

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat
exercises.description: This movement starts with your hands behind and legs in front. This allows you to keep your feet flat as you bring your hips forward to meet them.

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Quadruped Wag Tail
exercises.description: This sidebending motion activates your core in a unique way, the muscles alongside your spine and obliques are used and prepare them well

how_to: In table top, shift the hips side to side — like a dog wagging its tail.
The spine side-bends as the hips move left and right.
key_cues: Keep the movement rhythmic and relaxed.
Try to feel the side-bend through the whole spine, not just the lower back.
Great for activating the lateral core (obliques).

exercises.name: Table Top Twist
exercises.description: This motion takes you through spinal sidebending and rotation, along with a lateral stretch at the hips.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), thread one arm under the body.
Rotate the thoracic spine to lower that shoulder toward the floor.
Return and repeat on the other side.
key_cues: The rotation is thoracic — keep the lower back still.
Let the shoulder lower as far as comfortable.
Breathe out as you thread under.

exercises.name: Seated Underswitch to Squat
exercises.description: This combo will have you pull your leg underneath you from a sitting position into a squat.

how_to: From a seated position, perform an underswitch (rotate hips to bring legs through under the body).
Land in a squat as the legs come through.
key_cues: The underswitch is a hip rotation — let the hips lead.
Keep one hand planted through the switch for support.
Aim to land softly in squat with feet flat.

exercises.name: Squat Leg Sweep
exercises.description: Squat Leg Sweep

how_to: From a deep squat, sweep one leg out to the side along the floor.
The leg extends and sweeps in an arc as you shift weight to the other side.
key_cues: Keep the planted foot flat and the planted knee tracking over toes.
The sweep is fluid — let momentum carry the leg.
Engage the inner thigh of the sweeping leg as it extends.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Combo B
exercises.description: This combo focuses on twisting the body while only using one hand and one leg.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

sessionName: Monkey 360, Around the World, and Twisted Sister (Rotation) Flow
sessionDescription: Practice getting upside down, walking around the world, and a full flow!

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Seated Twist
exercises.description: Rotation is another key to moving more comfortably. Activities that have you twist and turn feel so much better when your back can rotate well.

how_to: Sit tall, cross one leg over, place the foot flat on the floor.
Rotate the torso toward the bent knee, using the elbow for leverage.
key_cues: Lengthen the spine first — then rotate.
Breathe out to deepen the twist.
Keep both sitting bones on the floor.

exercises.name: Table Top Twist
exercises.description: This motion takes you through spinal sidebending and rotation, along with a lateral stretch at the hips.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), thread one arm under the body.
Rotate the thoracic spine to lower that shoulder toward the floor.
Return and repeat on the other side.
key_cues: The rotation is thoracic — keep the lower back still.
Let the shoulder lower as far as comfortable.
Breathe out as you thread under.

exercises.name: Monkey 360
exercises.description: This movement combines the Monkey 180 along with the Squat Leg Sweep to give you a brand new rotational movement.

how_to: Perform the Monkey with a full 360° rotation.
Each complete rep returns you to the same facing direction.
key_cues: Combines Monkey 180 mechanics — do it twice to complete a full rotation.
Keep hips high through the rotation to generate the turn.
Use momentum from the first 180 to flow into the second.

exercises.name: Around The World
exercises.description: This squat variation will help range of motion within the ankles, hips, and twisting

how_to: A combination movement that links Bear, Monkey, Frogger, and Crab in a continuous loop.
Each movement transitions into the next — completing a 'world tour' of locomotion.
key_cues: Learn each transition individually before stringing them together.
Move slowly to start — speed comes with familiarity.
The Bear-to-Monkey and Frogger-to-Crab transitions are the trickiest — practice these.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Combo C
exercises.description: A very dynamic combo that will find you inverted and twisting.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Flow
exercises.description: This flow focuses on twists and rolls bringing your legs into the air.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

sessionName: Transformer, Bent Arm Floating Table Top, Straight Leg Frogger to Bent Arm Floating Table Top
sessionDescription: Practice both straight and bent leg movements in combination with each other.

exercises.name: Wrist Circles
exercises.description: Our wrists can take on a lot of force in bodyweight exercise, these circles help them to recover.

how_to: Extend arms forward or overhead.
Make slow circles with the wrists — clockwise then counterclockwise.
key_cues: Make the circles as large as possible to access full wrist range.
Do both directions — wrists often have asymmetric mobility.
Can be done in weight-bearing position (on hands) for greater effect.

exercises.name: Standing Twist
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation that helps release tension throughout the spine, shoulders, and arms.

how_to: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Rotate the torso side to side, arms swinging freely.
key_cues: Let the arms be completely relaxed — they swing from the torso rotation.
Keep feet flat and knees soft.
Gradually increase the rotation range as the spine warms up.

exercises.name: Extended Side Angle Pose
exercises.description: This classic yoga pose is great for lengthening the entire body as you sidebend and rotate through your back and into your hips and legs

how_to: Stand in a wide lunge, front knee at 90°.
Lower the front forearm to the front thigh (or hand to floor).
Reach the top arm overhead, creating a long diagonal line.
key_cues: Stack the hips — don't let the top hip collapse forward.
Reach actively through the top arm to lengthen the side body.
Keep the back leg strong and active.

exercises.name: Transformer
exercises.description: Transformer

how_to: A multi-position transition movement that flows between ground positions.
Typically: prone → side-lying → seated → supine and back.
key_cues: Move smoothly and continuously — no pausing between positions.
Use the hands for support only when necessary.
Think of it as a flowing shape-change, not a series of stops.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Floating Table Top
exercises.description: A bent arm version of a very familiar movement.

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Combo B
exercises.description: This challenging combo will test your ability to go from a straight arm position to bent arm hold.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

sessionName: Bent Arm Floating Table Top Air Twist, Side Crossing Bear, Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Flow
sessionDescription: Practice some familiar movements and then put them together into a full flow.

exercises.name: A-Frame
exercises.description: This exercise improves your ability to reach overhead as well as lengthening the back of your legs from your hips to your heels.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), press hips up as high as possible.
Allow a slight bend in the knees.
Body forms an upside-down V (A-frame/downward dog shape).
key_cues: Push hips UP and BACK as far as possible.
Allow slight knee bend — don't force straight legs if hamstrings are tight.
Press the floor away with the hands — engage the scapulae.
This is the entry position before Bear and Frogger work.

exercises.name: Floating Table Top
exercises.description: This teaches you a stable back position with bracing. This is very important and becomes automatic as you practice and move on to locomotions

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Frog Stretch
exercises.description: Open up your hips for better mobility.

how_to: Start on hands and knees and spread knees wide apart.
Lower hips back and down between the knees.
Feet point out in line with the knees.
key_cues: Keep feet and ankles in line with knees — don't let them roll in.
Rock gently forward and back to explore the range.
Breathe and relax — let gravity increase the stretch over time.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Floating Table Top Air Twist
exercises.description: A dynamic and very fun twisting movement.

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Side Crossing Bear
exercises.description: A variation on the bear that will have you play with going sideways.

how_to: Start on all fours with arms straight and a slight bend in your knees.
Move your right hand and left foot forward together.
Then move your left hand and right foot forward — always opposite hand and foot.
Continue this contralateral pattern to travel forward or backward.
key_cues: Opposite hand and foot move together — right hand with left foot.
Keep hips low and spine long — don't let the lower back sag.
Push the floor away to engage your scapulae (shoulder blades).
Stay light on your hands — weight is distributed, not dumped.
common_mistakes: Moving same-side hand and foot together (same side crawl).
Letting the hips rise too high and losing spinal alignment.
Locking the elbows — keep a soft, springy arm.
modifications: If wrists are sore, try fists or push-up handles.
If the movement is too difficult, start with the A-Frame: push hips up high, slight knee bend, to open shoulders and straighten spine.
benefits: Scapular strength: concentric/isometric protraction, eccentric/isometric retraction.
Rotator cuff strength: eccentric internal rotation and concentric/isometric external rotation.
Spinal strength for isometric extension, rotation, and flexion.
Hamstring and calf flexibility.
Decompresses the spine due to the inverted position.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Combo C
exercises.description: Time to do some flying with this fun twisting combo.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Flow
exercises.description: The theme of this Flow is straight and bent arm control while transitioning from straight to bent leg movements.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

sessionName: Forearm Leg Thread, Knee Wiper to Kneeling Lunge, and Floating Table Top Underswitch
sessionDescription: Today you'll practice leg threads and knee wipers, working to put them together.

exercises.name: Standing Twist
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation that helps release tension throughout the spine, shoulders, and arms.

how_to: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Rotate the torso side to side, arms swinging freely.
key_cues: Let the arms be completely relaxed — they swing from the torso rotation.
Keep feet flat and knees soft.
Gradually increase the rotation range as the spine warms up.

exercises.name: General Kneeling Lunge
exercises.description: A cornerstone exercise for improving your hip flexor mobility as well as learning how to differentiate your spine, pelvis, and hip motions

how_to: Kneel with one knee on the floor, the other foot planted forward.
Shift hips forward gently to stretch the hip flexor of the back leg.
Keep torso upright.
key_cues: Posteriorly tilt the pelvis (tuck the tailbone) to increase the hip flexor stretch.
Don't lean forward — stay tall to maximize the stretch.
Breathe into the front of the hip and inner thigh.

exercises.name: Quadruped Wag Tail
exercises.description: This sidebending motion activates your core in a unique way, the muscles alongside your spine and obliques are used and prepare them well

how_to: In table top, shift the hips side to side — like a dog wagging its tail.
The spine side-bends as the hips move left and right.
key_cues: Keep the movement rhythmic and relaxed.
Try to feel the side-bend through the whole spine, not just the lower back.
Great for activating the lateral core (obliques).

exercises.name: Forearm Leg Thread
exercises.description: Practice pulling one leg under you while bracing yourself on your forearm.

how_to: From a ground position, thread one leg under the body supported on forearms.
The leg slides through the space between the arms and the floor.
key_cues: Keep forearms flat and elbows under shoulders.
The threading leg stays close to the floor.
Control the movement — no dropping or flopping.

exercises.name: Knee Wiper to Kneeling Lunge
exercises.description: Practice a twisting movement on your knees.

how_to: From seated, let knees fall side to side (wipers) to loosen hips.
Transition into a kneeling lunge from the last wiper position.
key_cues: The wipers should be relaxed — gravity drops the knees.
Transition smoothly from the last wiper into the lunge.
In the lunge: posteriorly tilt pelvis to deepen the hip flexor stretch.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Combo B
exercises.description: Combine a leg thread to end up in a kneeling lunge position.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.
Focus on controlled lowering and rising through various ground shapes.
key_cues: Slow and deliberate — resist gravity on the way down.
Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

sessionName: Forward Shoulder Roll, Bent Arm Baby Frogger, Knee Wiper, and Space Oddity (Ground Control) Flow
sessionDescription: After practicing and playing with some familiar movements you'll get to challenge yourself with a full flow.

exercises.name: Forward Shoulder Roll with Arm Thread
exercises.description: Learn a version of the forward shoulder roll where you thread your arm underneath you.

how_to: From kneeling or standing, tuck one arm under the body as you roll forward.
The threading arm guides you through the roll diagonally.
key_cues: Thread the arm fully — hand reaches toward the opposite hip.
Roll on the back of the shoulder, not the point.
Tuck chin before initiating.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Baby Frogger
exercises.description: This baby frogger variation will have you bend your arms midway through the movement

how_to: A compact Frogger variation with bent elbows throughout.
Elbows stay bent as you shift weight forward and hop feet toward hands.
key_cues: Bent arms lower the center of gravity — feels more stable for beginners.
Elbows stay close to the body — don't flare them out.
Same pulling mechanic as the standard Frogger.
benefits: Elbow strength.
Spinal strength and controlled mobility.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Combo C
exercises.description: Work on connecting a forward roll directly into a Bent Arm Frogger.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.
Focus on controlled lowering and rising through various ground shapes.
key_cues: Slow and deliberate — resist gravity on the way down.
Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Flow
exercises.description: This flow focuses on getting low to the ground and transitioning from side to side.

Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

sessionName: Merry Go Round, Superhero, and a Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo
sessionDescription: After practicing the Merry Go Round and the Superhero you'll get to work on a combo.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Fold
exercises.description: This exercise keeps your back in a good strong position and teaches you how to stretch your legs without bending and straining your back

how_to: Sit with legs extended straight in front.
Fold forward from the hips — reach toward the feet.
key_cues: Fold from the HIPS, not the lower back — maintain a long spine as long as possible.
Let gravity increase the stretch — don't force the fold.
Flex the feet (toes toward you) to increase the hamstring stretch.

exercises.name: Seated Twist
exercises.description: Rotation is another key to moving more comfortably. Activities that have you twist and turn feel so much better when your back can rotate well.

how_to: Sit tall, cross one leg over, place the foot flat on the floor.
Rotate the torso toward the bent knee, using the elbow for leverage.
key_cues: Lengthen the spine first — then rotate.
Breathe out to deepen the twist.
Keep both sitting bones on the floor.

exercises.name: Kneeling Back Flexion to Prone Lying Back Extension
exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).
Then lie prone (face down) and press up into a cobra/back extension.
Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Full Merry Go Round
exercises.description: Full Merry Go Round

how_to: A complete 360° rotation around a fixed hand.
Combines the basic Merry Go Round with a full circle.
key_cues: Keep moving — don't stop mid-circle.
Let the hips drive the arc.
Keep the pivot hand strong and wrist stacked.

exercises.name: Superhero
exercises.description: Practice a hold in order to strengthen your lower back

how_to: A dynamic movement flowing between prone (chest down) positions.
Often involves pressing up from prone, transitioning over hands.
key_cues: Stay fluid — connect each position without pausing.
Engage the core to support the lower back in prone positions.
Move with intention — each shape should be clear before moving to the next.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo B
exercises.description: Practice controlled spins and rotations going from a supine to prone position.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

sessionName: Corkscrew, Prone Pull to Squat, Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow
sessionDescription: Today you'll get to work on putting the Corkscrew and Prone Pull to Squat together and finish up with Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow

exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).
Then lie prone (face down) and press up into a cobra/back extension.
Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Wrists - Palms Down, Fingers Facing Knees
exercises.description: Get your wrists ready to safely bear some weight.

how_to: Kneel or stand and place hands on the floor with palms down, fingers pointing back toward your knees.
Gently shift weight forward and backward over the hands.
key_cues: Go slowly — this targets the commonly tight wrist extensors.
Breathe into any tightness — don't force.
Work incrementally closer to the knees as flexibility improves.

exercises.name: Modified Pigeon Stretch
exercises.description: Moving your hips freely comes primarily from the flexibility of your hip flexors and rotators. This exercise works those deep rotators well

how_to: From a lunge, bring the front shin across the body (shin parallel to the front of the mat).
Lower the hips toward the floor.
Can be done lying on back (figure-four / supine pigeon) if floor pigeon is too intense.
key_cues: The deeper the shin angle (more parallel), the more intense the stretch.
Keep hips square — don't let one side roll up.
Breathe into the outer hip and glute.

exercises.name: Corkscrew
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation initiated with the legs.

how_to: A spiraling transition through multiple planes — typically from standing or kneeling down to the floor and back up.
key_cues: Use rotation to guide the descent — don't just fall.
Each spiral should feel connected and controlled.
The return journey is as important as the descent.

exercises.name: Prone Position Pull to Squat
exercises.description: A challenging movement to help improve your pulling strength.

how_to: Start lying face down.
Use arms and core to pull the body forward and up into a squat position.
key_cues: Initiate with the arms — pull, don't push.
Keep the core engaged throughout the pull.
Land the squat with feet flat and hips low.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo C
exercises.description: Practice going from prone to supine and return before pulling into a squat.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow
exercises.description: A flow designed to explore twisting from supine to prone position while opening and closing the body.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

sessionName: Long Leg Monkey, Deep Lunge, Twist to Deep Lunge
sessionDescription: Practice the Long Leg Monkey and Deep Lunge then play around with combining the two movements.

exercises.name: General Kneeling Lunge

how_to: Kneel with one knee on the floor, the other foot planted forward.
Shift hips forward gently to stretch the hip flexor of the back leg.
Keep torso upright.
key_cues: Posteriorly tilt the pelvis (tuck the tailbone) to increase the hip flexor stretch.
Don't lean forward — stay tall to maximize the stretch.
Breathe into the front of the hip and inner thigh.

exercises.name: Half Cossack Reach
exercises.description: This upper and lower body coordination movement combines a lateral trunk stretch with the Cossack Squat.

how_to: Stand in a wide stance.
Shift weight to one side into a lateral lunge, extending the other leg straight.
Reach the arm of the extended-leg side across the body or overhead.
key_cues: Keep the heel of the bent-knee leg flat on the floor.
The extended leg stays long — flex that foot.
The reach amplifies the side-body and inner-thigh stretch.

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat
exercises.description: This movement starts with your hands behind and legs in front. This allows you to keep your feet flat as you bring your hips forward to meet them.

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Basic Long Leg Monkey
exercises.description: In this variation of the Monkey you will extend your leg and while staying low and transition laterally into a squat via the Monkey.

how_to: Perform the Monkey with straight legs throughout.
Legs stay extended as you hop sideways.
key_cues: Requires hamstring flexibility to keep legs straight in lateral movement.
Drive the hip crease forward as you shift weight onto hands.
Don't compensate by bending the knees — work the range gradually.
benefits: All Basic Monkey benefits plus increased hip strength and mobility.

exercises.name: Deep Lunge
exercises.description: Variation of a general lunge that will have your leg extended in back.

how_to: Step into a long lunge with the back knee on the floor.
Sink the hips as low as possible toward the floor.
key_cues: Front shin should be vertical — knee over ankle.
Drive the back hip toward the floor.
Arms can reach overhead to increase the stretch through the hip flexor and spine.

exercises.name: Big In Japan (Sumo) Combo B
exercises.description: Combine familiar leg movements while staying close to the floor

how_to: A wide-stance, sumo-focused combination incorporating deep hip mobility movements.
Typically includes Sumo Frogger, Single Leg Sumo Squat, and related wide-stance flows.
key_cues: Wide stance throughout — maintain groin engagement.
Keep knees tracking over toes even in the wide positions.
Think of the sumo stance as a stable, powerful base.

sessionName: Duck Walk, High Monkey Long Step, and Big In Japan (Sumo) Flow
sessionDescription: After practicing and playing with some familiar movements it's time to put it all together into a full flow.

exercises.name: Half Cossack Reach
exercises.description: This upper and lower body coordination movement combines a lateral trunk stretch with the Cossack Squat.

how_to: Stand in a wide stance.
Shift weight to one side into a lateral lunge, extending the other leg straight.
Reach the arm of the extended-leg side across the body or overhead.
key_cues: Keep the heel of the bent-knee leg flat on the floor.
The extended leg stays long — flex that foot.
The reach amplifies the side-body and inner-thigh stretch.

exercises.name: General Kneeling Lunge
exercises.description: A cornerstone exercise for improving your hip flexor mobility as well as learning how to differentiate your spine, pelvis, and hip motions

how_to: Kneel with one knee on the floor, the other foot planted forward.
Shift hips forward gently to stretch the hip flexor of the back leg.
Keep torso upright.
key_cues: Posteriorly tilt the pelvis (tuck the tailbone) to increase the hip flexor stretch.
Don't lean forward — stay tall to maximize the stretch.
Breathe into the front of the hip and inner thigh.

exercises.name: Frog Stretch
exercises.description: Open up your hips for better mobility.

how_to: Start on hands and knees and spread knees wide apart.
Lower hips back and down between the knees.
Feet point out in line with the knees.
key_cues: Keep feet and ankles in line with knees — don't let them roll in.
Rock gently forward and back to explore the range.
Breathe and relax — let gravity increase the stretch over time.

exercises.name: Full Duck Walk
exercises.description: Full Duck Walk

how_to: Walk forward in a deep squat position — thighs parallel to floor or lower.
Take small steps while maintaining the squat depth.
key_cues: Keep heels flat — this demands ankle and hip mobility.
Stay upright — resist the urge to fold the torso forward.
Take small, controlled steps rather than waddling widely.

exercises.name: High Monkey Long Step
exercises.description: This variation of the High Monkey will have you step through to finish in a deep sumo squat.

how_to: Perform the Monkey with more weight shifted onto hands, elevating hips higher.
Approaches a pike-like position at peak of weight shift.
key_cues: Drive shoulders over the hands.
Keep core braced to control the elevated position.
Handstand prep component — practice the shoulder-over-wrist stacking.

exercises.name: Big In Japan (Sumo) Combo C
exercises.description: This combination will take your hips from low to high to in-between. :)

how_to: A wide-stance, sumo-focused combination incorporating deep hip mobility movements.

key_cues: Wide stance throughout — maintain groin engagement.

sessionName: Monkey 360, Around the World, and Twisted Sister (Rotation) Flow
sessionDescription: Practice getting upside down, walking around the world, and a full flow!

exercises.name: Walk Into Squat
exercises.description: This movement starts with your hands behind and legs in front. This allows you to keep your feet flat as you bring your hips forward to meet them.

how_to: Start with hands behind you on the ground, legs extended in front.
Walk the hands toward the feet while keeping feet flat.
Bring hips forward to meet the feet as you rise into squat.
key_cues: Keep feet flat throughout — don't let heels rise.
Let the knees track over the toes as you come up.
Good mobility warm-up for ankles, calves, and hips.

exercises.name: Seated Twist
exercises.description: Rotation is another key to moving more comfortably. Activities that have you twist and turn feel so much better when your back can rotate well.

how_to: Sit tall, cross one leg over, place the foot flat on the floor.
Rotate the torso toward the bent knee, using the elbow for leverage.
key_cues: Lengthen the spine first — then rotate.
Breathe out to deepen the twist.
Keep both sitting bones on the floor.

exercises.name: Table Top Twist
exercises.description: This motion takes you through spinal sidebending and rotation, along with a lateral stretch at the hips.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), thread one arm under the body.
Rotate the thoracic spine to lower that shoulder toward the floor.
Return and repeat on the other side.
key_cues: The rotation is thoracic — keep the lower back still.
Let the shoulder lower as far as comfortable.
Breathe out as you thread under.

exercises.name: Monkey 360
exercises.description: This movement combines the Monkey 180 along with the Squat Leg Sweep to give you a brand new rotational movement.

how_to: Perform the Monkey with a full 360° rotation.
Each complete rep returns you to the same facing direction.
key_cues: Combines Monkey 180 mechanics — do it twice to complete a full rotation.
Keep hips high through the rotation to generate the turn.
Use momentum from the first 180 to flow into the second.

exercises.name: Around The World
exercises.description: This squat variation will help range of motion within the ankles, hips, and twisting

how_to: A combination movement that links Bear, Monkey, Frogger, and Crab in a continuous loop.
Each movement transitions into the next — completing a 'world tour' of locomotion.
key_cues: Learn each transition individually before stringing them together.
Move slowly to start — speed comes with familiarity.
The Bear-to-Monkey and Frogger-to-Crab transitions are the trickiest — practice these.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Combo C
exercises.description: A very dynamic combo that will find you inverted and twisting.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

exercises.name: Twisted Sister (Rotation) Flow
exercises.description: This flow focuses on twists and rolls bringing your legs into the air.

how_to: A rotation-focused combination linking movements through spinal rotation.
Typically includes Monkey 360, Around the World, and other rotation-heavy transitions.
key_cues: Let the hips initiate every rotation — don't lead with the shoulders.
Stay low and close to the ground through transitions.
Control the landing after each rotation before initiating the next.

sessionName: Bent Arm Floating Table Top Air Twist, Side Crossing Bear, Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Flow
sessionDescription: Practice some familiar movements and then put them together into a full flow.

exercises.name: A-Frame
exercises.description: This exercise improves your ability to reach overhead as well as lengthening the back of your legs from your hips to your heels.

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), press hips up as high as possible.
Allow a slight bend in the knees.
Body forms an upside-down V (A-frame/downward dog shape).
key_cues: Push hips UP and BACK as far as possible.
Allow slight knee bend — don't force straight legs if hamstrings are tight.
Press the floor away with the hands — engage the scapulae.
This is the entry position before Bear and Frogger work.

exercises.name: Floating Table Top
exercises.description: This teaches you a stable back position with bracing. This is very important and becomes automatic as you practice and move on to locomotions

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Frog Stretch
exercises.description: Open up your hips for better mobility.

how_to: Start on hands and knees and spread knees wide apart.
Lower hips back and down between the knees.
Feet point out in line with the knees.
key_cues: Keep feet and ankles in line with knees — don't let them roll in.
Rock gently forward and back to explore the range.
Breathe and relax — let gravity increase the stretch over time.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Floating Table Top Air Twist
exercises.description: A dynamic and very fun twisting movement.

how_to: From a seated position, place hands behind you and feet flat on the floor.
Press hips up into a reverse table top — body forms a flat table shape.
Hold position briefly before lowering back down.
key_cues: Hips should be level with shoulders — don't let them sag.
Drive through heels and hands equally.
Pull shoulders down away from ears (scapular depression).
This is the foundational prep movement before full Frogger work.

exercises.name: Side Crossing Bear
exercises.description: A variation on the bear that will have you play with going sideways.

how_to: Start on all fours with arms straight and a slight bend in your knees.
Move your right hand and left foot forward together.
Then move your left hand and right foot forward — always opposite hand and foot.
Continue this contralateral pattern to travel forward or backward.
key_cues: Opposite hand and foot move together — right hand with left foot.
Keep hips low and spine long — don't let the lower back sag.
Push the floor away to engage your scapulae (shoulder blades).
Stay light on your hands — weight is distributed, not dumped.
common_mistakes: Moving same-side hand and foot together (same side crawl).
Letting the hips rise too high and losing spinal alignment.
Locking the elbows — keep a soft, springy arm.
modifications: If wrists are sore, try fists or push-up handles.
If the movement is too difficult, start with the A-Frame: push hips up high, slight knee bend, to open shoulders and straighten spine.
benefits: Scapular strength: concentric/isometric protraction, eccentric/isometric retraction.
Rotator cuff strength: eccentric internal rotation and concentric/isometric external rotation.
Spinal strength for isometric extension, rotation, and flexion.
Hamstring and calf flexibility.
Decompresses the spine due to the inverted position.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Combo C
exercises.description: Time to do some flying with this fun twisting combo.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

exercises.name: Sledgehammer (Strength and Power) Flow
exercises.description: The theme of this Flow is straight and bent arm control while transitioning from straight to bent leg movements.

how_to: A strength-focused combination emphasizing powerful, forceful movements.
Typically includes Bear, Crab, and power-driven transitions.
key_cues: Generate force intentionally — these moves should feel strong.
Keep the core braced tight through power transitions.
Recover fully between reps — quality over speed.

exercises.description: Learn a version of the forward shoulder roll where you thread your arm underneath you.

how_to: From kneeling or standing, tuck one arm under the body as you roll forward.
The threading arm guides you through the roll diagonally.
key_cues: Thread the arm fully — hand reaches toward the opposite hip.
Roll on the back of the shoulder, not the point.
Tuck chin before initiating.

exercises.name: Bent Arm Baby Frogger
exercises.description: This baby frogger variation will have you bend your arms midway through the movement

how_to: A compact Frogger variation with bent elbows throughout.
Elbows stay bent as you shift weight forward and hop feet toward hands.
key_cues: Bent arms lower the center of gravity — feels more stable for beginners.
Elbows stay close to the body — don't flare them out.
Same pulling mechanic as the standard Frogger.
benefits: Elbow strength.
Spinal strength and controlled mobility.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Combo C
exercises.description: Work on connecting a forward roll directly into a Bent Arm Frogger.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.
Focus on controlled lowering and rising through various ground shapes.
key_cues: Slow and deliberate — resist gravity on the way down.
Each position should be clear and intentional before moving to the next.
Ground contact is a feature, not a failure — use the floor.

exercises.name: Space Oddity (Ground Control) Flow
exercises.description: This flow focuses on getting low to the ground and transitioning from side to side.

how_to: A ground-control-focused combination emphasizing slow, deliberate floor transitions.

sessionName: Corkscrew, Prone Pull to Squat, Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow
sessionDescription: Today you'll get to work on putting the Corkscrew and Prone Pull to Squat together and finish up with Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow.

exercises.name: Kneeling Back Flexion to Prone Lying Back Extension
exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).
Then lie prone (face down) and press up into a cobra/back extension.
Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Wrists - Palms Down, Fingers Facing Knees
exercises.description: Get your wrists ready to safely bear some weight.

how_to: Kneel or stand and place hands on the floor with palms down, fingers pointing back toward your knees.
Gently shift weight forward and backward over the hands.
key_cues: Go slowly — this targets the commonly tight wrist extensors.
Breathe into any tightness — don't force.
Work incrementally closer to the knees as flexibility improves.

exercises.name: Modified Pigeon Stretch
exercises.description: Moving your hips freely comes primarily from the flexibility of your hip flexors and rotators. This exercise works those deep rotators well

how_to: From a lunge, bring the front shin across the body (shin parallel to the front of the mat).
Lower the hips toward the floor.
Can be done lying on back (figure-four / supine pigeon) if floor pigeon is too intense.
key_cues: The deeper the shin angle (more parallel), the more intense the stretch.
Keep hips square — don't let one side roll up.
Breathe into the outer hip and glute.

exercises.name: Corkscrew
exercises.description: This is a full body rotation initiated with the legs.

how_to: A spiraling transition through multiple planes — typically from standing or kneeling down to the floor and back up.
key_cues: Use rotation to guide the descent — don't just fall.
Each spiral should feel connected and controlled.
The return journey is as important as the descent.

exercises.name: Prone Position Pull to Squat
exercises.description: A challenging movement to help improve your pulling strength.

how_to: Start lying face down.
Use arms and core to pull the body forward and up into a squat position.
key_cues: Initiate with the arms — pull, don't push.
Keep the core engaged throughout the pull.
Land the squat with feet flat and hips low.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo C
exercises.description: Practice going from prone to supine and return before pulling into a squat.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Flow
exercises.description: A flow designed to explore twisting from supine to prone position while opening and closing the body.

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).
Think of the body expanding and contracting rhythmically.
key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.
Then fully compress: tuck tight, round the spine.
Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.

exercises.name: A-Frame

how_to: From table top (hands and knees), press hips up as high as possible.
Allow a slight bend in the knees.

Allow slight knee bend — don't force straight legs if hamstrings are tight.
Press the floor away with the hands — engage the scapulae.

key_cues: Thread the arm fully — hand reaches toward the opposite hip.
Roll on the back of the shoulder, not the point.
Tuck chin before initiating.

exercises.name: Kneeling Back Flexion to Prone Lying Back Extension
exercises.description: Loosen up your back's basic flexion and extension ranges.

how_to: Start kneeling and fold forward into child's pose (back flexion).

Move between the two positions slowly.
key_cues: In child's pose: reach arms long, let the spine decompress.
In back extension: keep the glutes relaxed — extension from the thoracic spine.
Breathe deeply in each position.

exercises.name: Wrists - Palms Down, Fingers Facing Knees
exercises.description: Get your wrists ready to safely bear some weight.

how_to: Kneel or stand and place hands on the floor with palms down, fingers pointing back toward your knees.
Gently shift weight forward and backward over the hands.
key_cues: Go slowly — this targets the commonly tight wrist extensors.

exercises.name: Modified Pigeon Stretch

exercises.name: Corkscrew

how_to: A spiraling transition through multiple planes — typically from standing or kneeling down to the floor and back up.

exercises.name: Prone Position Pull to Squat

how_to: Start lying face down.
Use arms and core to pull the body forward and up into a squat position.
key_cues: Initiate with the arms — pull, don't push.
Keep the core engaged throughout the pull.
Land the squat with feet flat and hips low.

exercises.name: Pump Up The Jam (Extension and Compression) Combo C

how_to: A combination alternating between extension (opening up, arching) and compression (folding, tucking).

key_cues: Exaggerate the extension: reach long, open the chest.

Move between the two with a clear rhythm — like breathing.
